title: Accessibilizer conversion event 1.0
description: One durable, metadata-only conversion event. The bundle's conversion-events.jsonl holds one such object per line, in order. This contract pins the core envelope; later releases may add fields, so unknown properties are permitted, but the log never carries a credential, API key, authorization header, base64 image, Source PDF text, prompt, raw request or response body, model-produced Semantic Layer content, or hidden reasoning.

schema_version.description: The event/schema contract version. Consumers should accept additive fields within a major version.

timestamp.description: UTC time the event was recorded, to the second.

stage.description: The major stage the event belongs to (e.g. provider-capability, source-preflight, page-recognition, provider-reconstruction, review-record, pdf-authoring, internal-checks, visual-comparison, verapdf-validation, bundle-publication).

state.description: Lifecycle state. Heartbeats are terminal feedback only and are never persisted here.

elapsed_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds the operation took, present on completion.

page.description: The current source page, for per-page stages.

page_count.description: The count of selected pages, for per-page stages.

request.description: The provider request number, for provider events.

request_total.description: The estimated total provider request count at the time of the event.

purpose.description: The purpose of a provider request (capability-check, page-reconstruction, region-verification).

endpoint.description: The configured provider base URL (credential-free by construction).

model.description: The configured exact provider model identifier.

token_usage.description: Provider-reported token counts for the request, when supplied.

attempt.description: The upcoming retry attempt number, on a retrying event.

delay.description: The backoff delay in seconds before the retry, on a retrying event.

detail.description: A short, safe operational detail (for example a response-body-free retry reason).

resume_command.description: The exact command that resumes an interrupted conversion, on an interrupted event.
